//: Appears in the refugee center as a source of missions and info.  Faction critical.
//2: Possibly should change to NPC_MISSION_SHOPKEEP and sell medical supplies.

responses.text: Heard anything about the outside world?
Can I do anything for you?

dynamic_line: I'm a doctor, one of the several at the outpost.  We were the lucky ones.  Came here right went things started to go wrong, never left.

responses.text: So what are you doing right now?

dynamic_line: The Old Guard--that's what's left of the feds--drafted me when they arrived a while ago and set me up here to screen any new arrivals for infection risks.  Can't be too paranoid these days.  Sad to have to turn people away, but I like the assignment for the chance to get news about the outside world.

Sightings of unusual living dead or new mutations.  The more we know about what's happening, the closer we can get to a treatment or maybe even a cure.  It's a long shot, but you have hope to survive.
This is no classic zombie outbreak.  The dead seem to be getting stronger as the days go on.  Some survivors too, come in here with… adaptations.  Maybe they're related.
We can't.  There's nothing we can spare to sell and I've got no budget to buy from you.  I don't suppose you want to donate?

description: Find a <color_light_blue>microcentrifuge</color>. The doctor knows of a <color_red>medical supply drop</color> where one could likely be found.

dialogue.describe: It could be very informative to perform an analysis of zombie blood…
dialogue.offer: We don't have the equipment for real analysis here so I'll need you to get some.  Critically, I'm missing a microcentrifuge with which I could perform an analysis of zombie blood.  I need you to procure a microcentrifuge and bring it to me.
dialogue.accepted: Excellent.  I happen to know of the location of a nearby supply drop of medical equipment, which likely contains a microcentrifuge.  Otherwise, you should try your luck at research locations.
dialogue.rejected: Are you sure?  The scientific value of that blood data could be priceless…
dialogue.advice: The supply drop should be unguarded, but watch your back.
dialogue.inquire: Well, do you have the microcentrifuge yet?
dialogue.success: Excellent!  This may be the key to removing the infection.  I don't have much to spare, but take this as thanks.
dialogue.success_lie: Wait, this isn't a centrifuge!  Liar!
dialogue.failure: What a shame, that equipment could have proved invaluable…

description: Find a <color_light_blue>workstation console</color> on the first basement floor of the <color_red>science lab</color> and download its data onto a USB drive.

dialogue.offer: Thanks to the microcentrifuge you found, I was able to perform an analysis of zombie blood.  It seems to contain an exotic substance that I am unfortunately unable to identify.  We are not yet out of options though.  Right before the start of all of this, I was under orders to immediately drive any valuable samples to the nearest secure laboratory.  I have the destination address.  Go there, get in, find a workstation, and take any data you can on a USB drive.
dialogue.accepted: Great!  I've mapped out a route to the address.  Most workstations should be on the first basement floor.
dialogue.rejected: Can't blame you, but come back if you change your mind.
dialogue.advice: These labs are usually locked, you may want to try and buy an acetylene torch from the merchant across from me.  Otherwise, there is a high likelihood you could find a science ID on deceased scientists.  Also, make sure you practice your computer skills before you attempt to hack the workstations, they can have dangerous anti-intruder systems.
dialogue.success: Thanks!  This data looks damaged, but maybe I can make something out of it. Also, since you've proven to be capable, I want to give you this equipment. The Old Guard issued it to me when they dropped by with the representative a while ago, but I'm not much of a fighter. I'm sure you could put it to good use though.
dialogue.success_lie: What good does this do us?
dialogue.failure: It was a lost cause anyways…

description: Find a <color_light_blue>storage archive console</color> on the fourth basement floor of the <color_red>cold science lab</color> and download its data onto a USB drive.

dialogue.offer: So there looks to be months, maybe years of experiments, and that data set must be huge.  Database servers massive enough to house it would overheat running on emergency power.  But I did find communications from a lab that had some kind of freezing portal open during the Cataclysm, sending everything to subzero temperatures.  I bet the archives inside that lab are still working.
dialogue.accepted: Great!  I've mapped out a route.  Bundle up, it gets colder the deeper you go and it looks like the archives were on the fourth basement level.
dialogue.rejected: Can't blame you, but come back if you change your mind.
dialogue.advice: That lab is going to start freezing and just get colder the deeper you go.  You'll really need special equipment to survive that far down.  Bring back anything you find on a USB drive.
dialogue.success: Thanks!  This is a lot of data to go through.  Take this clothing as compensation, it should be helpful if you ever need to go into one of those labs again.
dialogue.success_lie: What good does this do us?
dialogue.failure: It was a lost cause anyways…

description: Find the <color_light_blue>foreman</color> in the <color_red>microlab</color> and obtain info about the train network.

dialogue.offer: In the data we found a major contract for tunneling and train equipment, ordered a long time ago.  It's the best lead we have.  If these records are correct, it seems this equipment was used to build fully underground 'microlabs' where less hazardous research was carried out.  Luckily, I've identified a microlab that was only just finished before the Cataclysm, enough so that the construction foreman was likely still present.  If anyone carried information about the train networks, it's him.  I want you to go there and retrieve this information.
dialogue.rejected: Can't blame you, but come back if you change your mind.
dialogue.advice: I would recommend finding a subway station or other form of underground access and then navigating along the subway lines until you reach the lab.  Keep in mind that less hazardous research doesn't mean less hazardous for you.  These microlabs are likely crawling with zombies and automated defenses, and their tight and winding corridors mean you'll have to be extra careful.
dialogue.success: Fantastic!  I should be able to reconstruct what cargo moved between which labs.  I wonder what was really going on down there.
dialogue.success_lie: What good does this do us?
dialogue.failure: It was a lost cause anyways…

description: Find and obtain the <color_light_blue>classified lab reports</color> in the <color_red>trans-coast logistics center</color>.

dialogue.offer: The train network information revealed a completely separate secret train network deeper than the normal subway system that seems to be used exclusively for transport between labs.  Not only that, but it also revealed a network of logistics centers.  You know those trans-coast logistics centers, the big warehouses from the company that promised you same-day shipping?  It turns out those are actually fronts used to facilitate the mass shipment of supplies and the conduction of classified research!  If any place holds the key to unlocking the mystery of how and why this all happened, it is almost certainly locked within one of those centers!  I've identified the center that seems to have been the most critical for the region, and I need you to travel to it and find any information that points you to what could possibly have caused all of this!
dialogue.rejected: Can't blame you, but come back if you change your mind.
dialogue.advice: This may be the most dangerous lab you've visited yet.  If I recall correctly, the main entrance is heavily defended by rifle caliber turrets and robots, not to mention the swarms of zombies and other abominations you'll likely find inside.  An intelligent course of action may be to find an entrance to this secret train network, likely located in a lab or microlab at a depth equivalent to the fourth basement floor, and navigate to the logistics center along it.
dialogue.success: Fantastic!  This is exactly the data I needed, and I have a feeling that we will soon uncover a way to finally halt this Cataclysm...
dialogue.success_lie: What good does this do us?
dialogue.failure: It was a lost cause anyways…
